12.reason: The condition checks scanf's return value against -1 (EOF), but the problem specifies that -1 is an operation code that terminates the sequence. The loop should break when op == -1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition should be != EOF, not != -1. The problem specifies -1 as the end-of-operations marker, but scanf returns EOF on end-of-file, not -1. This will cause incorrect loop termination.

18.reason: This else branch will execute for op == 0 and op == -1, but it should only execute for op == 0. When op == -1 (end of operations), the program should stop, not call pop().

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This else branch will execute for op == 0 and op == -1, but it should only execute for op == 0. When op == -1 (end of operations), the program should stop, not call pop().

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [else branch covers both op==0 and op==-1; for op==-1 it should not call pop; should use else if (op==0) or handle -1 separately]
